sections.paragraph:
εἰσήκω fut. εἰσήξω (no certain evidence of use in literary texts before imperial times: Aeschyl., Ag. 1181 [mss. FTr; Fraenkel reads ἐσάιξειν w. Bothe; s. F.’s comm. ad loc.]; Aristoph., Vesp. 606; and Dio Cassius 37, 32 all have been emended by scholars; BCH 6, 18 [II b.c.]; Sb 631d, 1) to arrive on a scene, enter the kgdm. of God 2 Cl 11:7.—DELG s.v. ἥκω.

fut. fut. = fut.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

comm. comm. = commentary, -aries, commentator(s)

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Dio Cassius Dio Cassius , s. Cass. Dio; II–III a.d.—List 5

BCH BCH = Bulletin de correspondance hellénique—List 6

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

kgdm. kgdm. = kingdom

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 294
